sections.heading: Gallons Per Square Foot Calculator
sections.content:
Instantly calculate gallons per square foot (gal per sq ft) for water, paint, or any liquid based on area and depth.

Use this advanced Gallons Per Square Foot Calculator to convert square feet to gallons, gallons to square feet, and determine how many gallons per square foot you need for construction, landscaping, irrigation, pools, concrete curing, waterproofing, or paint coverage.

sections.heading: How Many Gallons Are in a Square Foot?
sections.content:
A square foot measures area, while a gallon measures volume. You can only convert square feet to gallons when you include depth (height or thickness).

Formula:
Gallons = Square Feet × Depth (in feet) × 7.48052

Since:
1 cubic foot = 7.48052 gallons (US liquid gallons)

If you pour 1 inch of water over 1 square foot, you get:
1 sq ft × (1/12 ft) × 7.48052 = 0.623 gallons

1 inch of water per square foot equals 0.623 gallons.

This is the standard used in irrigation and rainfall calculations across the United States.

sections.heading: What is a Gallons Per Square Foot Calculator?
sections.content: A gallons per square foot calculator is a practical online tool that allows you to calculate the volume of liquid required to cover a given area. It is particularly useful for:

subSections.heading: Painters
subSections.content: Painters who need to estimate paint usage accurately for walls, ceilings, and surfaces.

subSections.heading: Gardeners and Landscapers
subSections.content: Gardeners or landscapers planning irrigation systems and water requirements.

subSections.heading: Construction Projects
subSections.content: Construction projects requiring water or other liquid materials for various applications.

subSections.heading: Key Calculations
subSections.content:
By inputting either the total gallons you have or the area in square feet, the calculator can instantly tell you:
• How many gallons per square foot are required
• Square footage to gallons conversion
• Gallons to square feet coverage

This eliminates guesswork and ensures efficient use of resources.

sections.heading: Conversion Table
sections.content: Here is a conversion table for quick reference:

subSections.heading: Gallons to Square Feet
subSections.content:
1 gallon = 0.133681 cubic feet
1 cubic foot = 7.48052 gallons

Square Feet = Gallons ÷ (Depth × 7.48052)

sections.heading: Why Gallons and Square Feet Cannot Be Directly Converted
sections.content:
A square foot measures area (ft²).
A gallon measures volume (liquid capacity).

To convert between them, you must include:
• Length
• Width
• Depth (height or thickness)

This creates cubic feet, which then convert to gallons.
This concept follows dimensional analysis used in physics and engineering.

sections.heading: How to Calculate Gallons Per Square Foot
sections.content:
Step 1: Calculate Area in Square Feet
Area = Length × Width
Example: 20 ft × 10 ft = 200 sq ft

Step 2: Convert Depth to Feet
If depth is in inches:
Depth (feet) = Inches ÷ 12
Example: 3 inches = 3 ÷ 12 = 0.25 ft

Step 3: Calculate Volume in Cubic Feet
Volume = Area × Depth
200 × 0.25 = 50 cubic feet

Step 4: Convert Cubic Feet to Gallons
Gallons = Cubic Feet × 7.48052
50 × 7.48052 = 374.026 gallons

Step 5: Find Gallons Per Square Foot
Gallons per sq ft = Total Gallons ÷ Area
374.026 ÷ 200 = 1.87 gal per sq ft

sections.heading: Gallons Per Square Foot Formula Summary

subSections.heading: Rearranged Formulas
subSections.content:
Convert gallons to square feet:
Square Feet = Gallons ÷ (Depth × 7.48052)

Calculate depth from gallons:
Depth = Gallons ÷ (Sq Ft × 7.48052)

subSections.heading: 1. Irrigation and Water Management
subSections.content:
Farmers calculate how many gallons of water per square foot when applying 1 inch of irrigation.

Since 1 inch per sq ft = 0.623 gallons:
For 1,000 sq ft:
1,000 × 0.623 = 623 gallons

This is widely used in USDA water planning.

subSections.heading: 2. Swimming Pools
subSections.content: Pool installers convert square footage and depth into gallons to size pumps and filters.

subSections.heading: 3. Concrete Slabs
subSections.content: Contractors calculate water curing volume per square foot.

subSections.heading: 4. Waterproofing and Sealants
subSections.content: Manufacturers specify gallons per square foot coverage.

subSections.heading: 5. Paint Coverage Calculations
subSections.content:
Most paint covers 250–400 square feet per gallon.

If 5 gallons cover 400 sq ft:
400 ÷ 5 = 80 sq ft per gallon

For 640 sq ft:
640 ÷ 80 = 8 gallons needed

sections.heading: Conversion Table: Gallons Per Square Foot by Depth
sections.content:
| Depth | Gallons per Square Foot |
|-------|-------------------------|
| 1 inch | 0.623 gal |
| 2 inches | 1.247 gal |
| 3 inches | 1.87 gal |
| 6 inches | 3.74 gal |
| 12 inches (1 ft) | 7.48 gal |

sections.heading: Common Conversions Users Search
sections.additionalContent: All follow the same formula with depth included.

subSections.heading: Example 1: 144 Square Feet at 2 Inches Deep
subSections.content:
Depth in feet: 2 ÷ 12 = 0.167 ft
Gallons: 144 × 0.167 × 7.48052 = 179.7 gallons

subSections.heading: Example 2: 500 Square Feet at 1 Inch Deep
Example 3: 1000 Sq Ft Lawn with 1 Inch Irrigation

sections.heading: Gallons of Water Per Square Foot
sections.content:
Many users ask:
• How many gallons of water are in a square foot?
• How many gallons per square foot of water?

Answer: It depends on depth.

At:
• 1 inch = 0.623 gallons
• 6 inches = 3.74 gallons
• 12 inches = 7.48 gallons

sections.heading: Gallons of Paint Per Square Foot
sections.content:
Paint coverage differs from water depth.

Instead of volume depth, manufacturers provide:
Square Feet per Gallon

Example: If 1 gallon covers 350 sq ft:
Gallons per sq ft = 1 ÷ 350 = 0.00285 gallons

sections.heading: Difference Between Water and Paint Calculations
list: Always check the label before calculating.

sections.heading: Square Feet to Gallons Calculator vs Gallons to Square Feet Calculator
sections.content:
Both tools use the same formula, reversed.

• Square feet → gallons requires depth.
• Gallons → square feet requires depth.
• Without depth, conversion is impossible.

sections.heading: Experience-Based Insights
list: Always add 5–10% extra gallons for waste.
Wind and evaporation reduce effective irrigation volume.
Paint on textured walls needs 15–20% extra.
sections.additionalContent: Professionals never calculate exact minimum values. They always add safety margins.

faq.q: How to calculate gallons per square foot?
faq.a: To calculate gallons per square foot, you must include depth because square feet measure area and gallons measure volume. Use this formula: Gallons = Square Feet × Depth (in feet) × 7.48052. If depth is in inches, divide inches by 12 first. For example, at 1 inch depth: 1 sq ft × (1 ÷ 12) × 7.48052 = 0.623 gallons

faq.q: How to calculate gallons of paint per square foot?
faq.a: Paint calculations work differently from water. Most paint covers 250–400 square feet per gallon depending on surface type. To calculate gallons of paint per square foot: Gallons per sq ft = 1 ÷ Coverage Rate. If 1 gallon covers 350 sq ft: 1 ÷ 350 = 0.00285 gallons per square foot. For best results, add 10–15% extra for textured or porous surfaces.

faq.q: How to calculate gallons of rainfall per square foot?
faq.a: Rainfall depth determines gallons per square foot. 1 inch of rain over 1 square foot equals 0.623 gallons. Formula: Gallons = Sq Ft × Rainfall (in inches ÷ 12) × 7.48052. For 1,000 sq ft during 1 inch rainfall: 1,000 × 0.0833 × 7.48052 = 623 gallons. This formula is widely used in irrigation planning and stormwater management.

faq.q: How many gallons are in 1 square foot?
faq.a: There is no fixed number because gallons measure volume and square feet measure area. At different depths: 1 inch deep → 0.623 gallons, 6 inches deep → 3.74 gallons, 12 inches deep → 7.48 gallons. Depth always determines the final answer.

faq.q: How many gallons of water per square foot at 1 inch deep?
faq.a: At exactly 1 inch depth: 1 square foot = 0.623 gallons. This value comes from: 1 cubic foot = 7.48052 gallons, 1 inch = 1/12 foot. This measurement is standard in agriculture and irrigation systems.

faq.q: How to convert gallons to square feet?
faq.a: To convert gallons to square feet, you must know the depth. Use this formula: Square Feet = Gallons ÷ (Depth × 7.48052). Example: If you have 100 gallons at 2 inches depth: Depth in feet = 2 ÷ 12 = 0.167, 100 ÷ (0.167 × 7.48052) = 80 square feet

faq.q: How many square feet does 5 gallons cover?
faq.a: For paint coverage: If 1 gallon covers 350 sq ft: 5 gallons cover: 5 × 350 = 1,750 square feet. Always check manufacturer coverage rates because coverage varies by brand and surface texture.
